{{Quote|Serve me well, Toth, and you'll hold a prominent place in our New Order.|Count Dooku, to Cavik Toth|<ref name="Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]''</ref>}}
'''Cavik Toth''' was a [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] who pledged the support of [[Sabaoth Squadron/Legends|Sabaoth Squadron]] to the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], becoming an early ally to [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] prior to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Cavik Toth. He might be more dangerous than ''me''.|[[Nym/Legends|Nym]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter" />}}
[[File:Dooku_and_Toth.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Count Dooku gives instructions to Cavik Toth.]]
Toth had spent many years working for various [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] groups, where he learned his tactical and combat skills. Eventually, he founded his own mercenary force, which was known as the [[Sabaoth Squadron/Legends|Sabaoth Squadron]]. Toth was placed in command of the [[trihexalon]] project carried out by a group of [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]] scientists led by [[Harro Ruuk]]. However, when Harro Ruuk was behind schedule, Toth took full command of the project, killing Harro and two of his assistants with a sample of the weapon. Once the chemical weapon was complete, Toth first tested the weapon on [[Point Modie]], a colony located in [[Maramere]]. The test was considered a success as it had killed hundreds of individuals. [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]], along with [[Reti]] and [[Sol Sixxa]], stopped the demonstration before any more damage could be done. Toth then planned to unleash it upon the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]]. However, the pirate captain [[Nym/Legends|Nym]], leader of the [[Lok Revenants/Legends|Lok Revenants]], arrived at [[Nod Kartha]] and [[Battle of Nod Kartha|destroyed]] the Hex factory, effectively stopping production. Toth then went to [[Lok/Legends|Lok]] when it was [[Battle of Lok (22 BBY)|attacked]] by Nym's pirates, who were trying to reclaim their planet. Cavik Toth escaped but left [[Bella (Twi'lek)|Bella]] behind, believing she could handle the pirate attack while he went to [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]] to help the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] in their losing [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|battle]] against the Republic. He was wrong, and Bella was killed.

Upon arrival at Geonosis, Toth's [[Hex fleet]] of [[Hex Deployer]]s was ambushed and destroyed by a [[Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|Jedi starfighter]] squadron led by Jedi Master Adi Gallia. Toth himself brought his [[Sabaoth destroyer|Sabaoth Destroyer]]s into battle. However, the capital ship turrets on all ships were destroyed, allowing the [[Mere cruiser]]s to destroy them one by one. Soon he brought his flagship into the battle, though let pride get the better of him—his ship's defenses were soon stripped, and his flagship suffered critical hull damage. He then engaged Adi Gallia in [[Starfighter combat/Legends|starfighter combat]].

Cavik Toth was killed above [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]] by Adi Gallia, while flying [[Cavik Toth's starfighter|his personal starfighter]], moments before his flagship exploded.

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Who would be evil enough to use a hex weapon on civilians?|[[Jinkins]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter" />}}
[[File:Toth_with_hex_sample.jpg|thumb|250px|Toth with a vial of trihexalon.]]
An ambitious young man, Toth was a natural leader and a confident warrior. These traits allowed him to control a sizable mercenary force. He was overconfident and persistent, traits that would ultimately lead to his downfall. His prime motivation was money, so he would pledge his services to the highest bidder; in this case, Count Dooku. Toth was known to punish his subordinates' incompetence with death, such as when [[Harro Ruuk]] delivered the hex weapon behind schedule.

==Behind the scenes==
Cavik Toth first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]'', a [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]]. He was voiced by [[Adrian Zmed]] and [[Amy Beth Christenson]] provided concept art in the character's design.

According to concept artwork produced by artist Christenson and released as bonus material in the ''Jedi Starfighter'' game, Toth's appearance was partially based on that of Game Designer [[Tim Longo]].
